title: Arenas, Pools, Slabs, and Fragmentation · Memory-Safety Defense
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar arenas, pools, slabs, and fragmentation decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for instrumented arena and object pool. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.
Bind every arena allocation to a phase or owner whose reset cannot race with surviving borrows.
Maintain free-list membership, generation, duplicate-release, and exhaustion invariants.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Arena Bump Invariant · changed case · Quiz Arena Bump Invariant
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz native-systems team handles memory case 5324. Produce bounded C evidence to maintain checked arena offset and high-water invariants; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant.
